titleStmt.title: Translation of BL Cotton Ch. xviii.22

div.p: Henry the earl, son of the king of Scots, to bishops, abbots, earls, justiciars, barons, sheriffs, officials, and all his sworn men, French and English, clerk and lay, future as well as present, of his whole land, greeting. Know that I have conceded Clerkington to God and to the church of St Mary at Haddington in perpetual alms with its right marches on either side of the the river just as William of Graham and Durand the sheriff, and Richard the clerk and Osbert the prior of Edinburgh [Holyrood], and Malbeth of Liberton and Gillanders son of Oggu led the way and went around, after Thorald the archdeacon determined the bounds with me at Peebles, and just as it was previously measured. I also concede to the aforesaid church one full toft next to the church in the toun of Haddington, and all teinds and ecclesiastical revenues from the whole of Haddingtonshire from mills as well as other things. I therefore will and firmly enjoin that the aforesaid church hold and have the aforenamed land, Clerkington, alone, and quit in relation to me and the thane and all others who shall have held the aforesaid land of Haddington of me and my heirs and of their heirs, and all other revenues and customs in teinds and alms as freely and honourably and quietly and fully as other alms in my whole land are well and fully and honourably and quietly able to be given and granted. These aforenamed alms I give and confirm by my charter for the sake of my own soul and of my father’s and mother’s, and the souls of my ancestors and successors. As witnesses: Engelram [Ingram] the chancellor, and William Maule, and Edmund son of Aimar, and Anselm nephew of Bishop John. At Haddington.
